message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>The Covid-19 pandemic has caused at more than 3 million deaths by Mai this year [1]. It had a significant impact on the daily life and the global economy [2]. The virus has since its first recorded outbreak in China [3] mutated into new strains [4]. The Nextstrain [5] project has so far been monitoring the evolution of the virus. At the same time we were developing in our lab the MNHN-Tree-Tools [6] toolkit, primarily for the investigation of DNA repeat sequences. We have further extended MNHN-Tree-Tools [6] to guide phylogenetics. As such the toolkit has evolved into a high performance code, allowing for a fast investigation of millions of sequences. Given the context of the pandemic it became evident that we will use our versatile tool to investigate the evolution of SARS-CoV-2 sequences. Our efforts have cumulated in this tutorial that we share with the scientific community.</jats:p>
title: Tutorial: Investigating SARS-CoV-2 evolution and phylogeny using MNHN-Tree-Tools

reference.unstructured: Owen Dyer . Covid-19: Study claims real global deaths are twice official figures. BMJ, 373, 2021.
